reviews.review_text: This game has been abandoned by the devs, no updates sicne october 2025 or communication
Massive potential and DEV did a runner with our money. same old story on sim games

Fast Food Simulator has a lot of potential, but right now it feels like a game that still needs time and development. I only play singleplayer, and while the core gameplay is enjoyable at first, you eventually start noticing how many systems feel incomplete or repetitive.
The idea itself is great. Running a fast food restaurant, preparing orders under pressure and managing the workflow can actually become really addictive. The game has that “just one more day” feeling that keeps you playing. But after several hours, the lack of content and polish becomes obvious.
Singleplayer especially needs more balancing and quality-of-life improvements. Doing everything alone can sometimes feel less challenging and more tedious. Better AI helpers, more progression depth, more recipes, random events, restaurant customization, and smoother mechanics would make a huge difference.
Despite the flaws, I still enjoyed the game because the foundation is genuinely good. You can tell the developers have a solid concept in their hands. If they continue updating it, listening to feedback, and expanding the gameplay, this could become one of the better restaurant simulators out there. Right now it feels more like an early access game with strong potential than a fully complete experience.

I can't recommend this game in it's current state because it seems the developers have gone AWOL, the game hasn't seen or even heard of an update for about 6 months. I don't usually call games abandoned, but 6 months with no news whatsoever is not reassuring. Save your money.
No updates or communications from the developer in several months. Looks like the developers abandoned this game.  Don't waste your money. This game will likely never be finished.

So, another scam artist with a game lost in EA. 
I already lost my money, all I can do is hope some of you can save yours.
This game is good, but they quit. it will not receive any more updates so the 'coming soon' is a lie. Only fun with friends. worth it

We were so close to greatness but the devs had to go and drop the game and spend all their money on blow, this could have been fantastic but no the bastards had to go and leave it behind like a prom night dumpster baby
This game is good, just wish that when hiring people I can hire a cook to atleast cook the meat, to make the burgers, and to make drinks.

It doesn't seem like they are ever planning to release this game out of the 'early access' stage. 
Judging from early access content, it doesn't feel like this game has gotten much love from the developers due to the fact that even the basic mechanics feel dodgy and quickly put together with no thought. That's how this whole game feels to me.
It left my friend and I feeling conflicted and confused as to whether we like this game or not - and we can usually make any game fun. I think that says it all.

Fast Food Simulater seems to be abandoned by devs. There was no update in 6 months & then an announcement to say they weren’t gone & it’s been another  6 months of nothing… 🤦‍♀️

They only have a few small jobs, leaving players to continuously do the bulk of the work on repeat. Also encountered a terrible glitches that made the workers stop doing there jobs, leading to failed orders, and once everything in our fridge disappeared after we just fully stocked it.

game has been abandoned. there is no way to contact the devs, there discord has been deleted as i checked today and they don't respond on the community posts not even to say the games not dead. steam will not refund you for this game so don't waste your time or money. it is a shame there was alot of potential here.

Game is abandoned.
Another 'Early Access' that got their money and left with an unfinished product.

The game had the potential to be good, but the last update/announcement was in September 2025. Unfortunately, it seems that it is now abandonware and I didn't notice until I checked today. A refund was denied since I had 4 hours in-game already.

Otherwise I had a bit of fun with a friend up until now, though soon we will reach the limit of content. 

I wouldn't recommend it for full price - 5$ at most. (and playing it with friends makes it more enjoyable/manageable).

Fast Food Simulator had a lot of potential at the beginning. The idea was great, and especially with friends, the kitchen chaos is genuinely fun at first. Unfortunately, the game now feels like an abandoned project.

There haven’t been any real updates for over a year, no new content, barely any communication, and many bugs or community requests were never addressed. After a few hours, it feels like you’ve already seen everything, and there’s almost no motivation left to continue playing.

That’s what makes it so disappointing, because the core concept is actually really good. With regular updates, this could have become an amazing co-op game. In its current state, I can only recommend it if you get it heavily discounted during a sale.

I still hope the developers eventually come back and bring the game back to life.

The idea is nice, but it has a lot of room for improvement and bugfixes however the devs seems to have abandoned it. Which is a shame, the game has potential but now it looks more of a cashgrab. Wouldn't recommend unless it is in good sale but honestly woulnd't spend more then €5 on it, then it might be nice to spend some evenings on with friends however keep in mind that the progression curve is not that steep and it might become boring quickly.

Looking past the obvious lack of updates and stuff, I think the gameplay is just kinda mediocre and needlessly complicated. 

The grilling is too specific: it works off of a percentage system, and if it goes beyond 105% or so, customers just refuse it entirely. The customers also ask for a specific amount of things, like two pickles and one onion, for example. It's a fast food restaurant, you should really just have a menu that they can only make small adjustments to, like no pickles, tomatoes, or onions. No person going to a fast food place would reasonably just walk out and leave their order behind because there was a tomato in their burger they didn't want. I also don't understand why they complain about the order the burger was built in, like, why do you care which bun is where? You're eating the whole thing regardless.

This game would be so much better if the cooking was more streamlined. They should really just make patties reach a fully cooked state and make it have a clear burn point where it's obvious it needs to go in the trash. Instead of requesting multiples of things, aside from patties, it should really just request that the burgers have a "serving" of ketchup, onion, tomato, etc. I don't want to deal with someone asking for four pickles, leaving, and wasting my time because I missed one. It's incredibly arbitrary that the customers demand perfection out of fast food, especially since they don't even pay upfront or throw away their own trash.

Maybe the devs will return with an update that can address these complaints, but for now, the game is rather unsatisfying in its delivery.

it's fun at the start but if you don't have a group to play with it's ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ♥♥♥♥. 
and there hasn't been an update for like a year it's sooo ♥♥♥♥ rn please devs update this

This game feels abandoned. The NPC's are so creepy and lifeless. The food orders are just so robotic and unrealistic. Your customers order the most dumb orders imaginable like... single patty 4 pickles. or a patty and 4 things of ketchup. It takes a long time to level up and everything just feels like such a boring grind. 
The interactions with customers are so unrealistic as well. There are too many limitations when it comes to having fun. Customization and personalization are behind a pay wall and the construction is very limited. I honestly find myself getting bored with friends. There is no music either. So we just play spotify to make up for it and even when we do WE STILL GET BORED.... I'm going to ask for a refund. I doubt I will get it but this feels abandoned. Last updated in 2025. Even with my friends this game just felt so uncanny and the opposite of fun. I do not recommend it. Stay away from this game.
The game is cool. Graphics are great and it is fun to play solo or with friends.
I am in love with simulator games! They are so fun and always a great time with my friends. I do hope this game gets more updates and expansions as it has so much potential for more.

Fun concept, somethings don't make sense, the coherence of some of the functions aren't realistic to a real fast food restaurant.  Thing is, the lack of cohesion is a sign of a bigger problem along side a lack of updates.  A lack of transparency on the progress being made or even simple engagement with community via their provided discord.  Seemingly, and I'm less invested in checking back religiously, but the word on the street is the Dev team have gone dead silent as evidenced by update time frames see below.

To the Devs:

Understanding the Early Access Program:

Early Access is a development model designed to let players participate in the evolution of a game before its "1.0" release. However, the program carries specific expectations and risks that are relevant to the current state of Fast Food Simulator:

* Iterative Development & Feedback: The primary goal is to gather community feedback to refine mechanics. When a simulator lacks "realistic cohesion" in its core loops, the Early Access period is intended to be the time when those functions are fixed. A lack of updates suggests this iterative loop has stalled.

* The Transparency Mandate: Steam’s guidelines encourage developers to be transparent about their roadmap. In Early Access, communication is often as important as the code itself. When a dev team goes "dead silent," it creates a vacuum where the community cannot distinguish between a long development cycle and a project that has been rescoped or abandoned.

* The "Playable State" Rule: While Early Access titles are unfinished, they are expected to be "worth the current value" of the build. If the gap between updates exceeds several months without a status report, the "live service" nature of the program is broken, leaving players with a static product rather than an evolving one.

* The 12-Month Red Flag: If an Early Access game goes 12 months without a formal update event, Steam adds a public note to the store page warning players that the game has not been updated in a while. Based on the October 2025 patch date, this title is approaching the window where the "Early Access" label transitions from a "collaboration" to a "risk."

Summary of Status
The build history shows a transition from bi-weekly/monthly updates in early 2025 to a total silence following the October patch. For a simulator relying on community engagement via Discord, this lack of cohesion between the dev roadmap and the player experience is currently the program's biggest hurdle and yours regarding a continued reputation. ⊹ ࣪ ˖ **Recommended** ˖ ࣪ ⊹

**Fast Food Simulator** was so much fun to play, especially with my friend Gaia. This game is chaotic, silly, and way more entertaining when you are trying to run a fast food place with someone else. ⋆˚꩜｡

The gameplay is all about taking orders, making food, serving customers, managing the restaurant, and trying to keep everything moving without the place falling apart. It has that fun simulator chaos where simple tasks somehow become stressful very quickly. ✧

Gaia and I had our own fast food slogan for our place called **Gurl Dinner**:
**“Where you can eat and slay.”** ˚₊‧꒰ა ☆ ໒꒱ ‧₊˚

Honestly, that alone made the game even better. We were serving food, causing chaos, and I may or may not have lovingly threatened customers if they did not pay enough. Very normal customer service behavior. ⟡

Playing with a friend made everything funnier, from trying to keep up with orders to messing around with customers and pretending we were running the most iconic fast food business ever.

Overall, **Fast Food Simulator** is a fun and chaotic simulator game that is best enjoyed with friends. If you like cooking games, managing orders, and turning your restaurant into a full comedy show, this one is worth playing. ✨

Fair warning: this game is considered dead. For what the game is in its current state, it is a decent game. It's fun to play with friends or solo. If purchasing, Expect for the game to be unfinished and never be finished. I would only recommend on sale, not full price. You do move the camera a lot, especially if your role is Cook. I recommend using the motion sickness option in the Settings so that you can simply move the mouse cursor on screen to move ingredients around on screen and place/grab patties. That's if you play with keyboard/mouse. Controller is only partially supported.

its a good game, if you want to add realism maybe put some expire date in meats and vegetables, and it will go bad if you just leave it in the open like its not in the freezer also add a dish washer so that we cant just re use used trays, it should be washed first before using again.  for level "simulation mode"

you can add day dots stickers or any form so the players can add used by dates in the products, "use first stickers" can also be handy

office can also use some interaction to manage the orders, you can transfer the ordering PC into the office, in that way, the manager can order stocks there, also in the delivery, its a good thing to add a cart or dolly so it will be easier to stock, 

i think when you remove the pc in the kitchen, you can add a dishwasher there, also interaction of the stove and other equipments would be nice like adjusting the power of fire so that the food will cook faster, and also upgrading equipments, like from the start we have a small dishwasher, you get what i mean, good game potential.

"Welcome to Beef Lips, home of the Beef Lips & buns! Can I take your order?" This game is hilarious with friends and a good change of pace for some. We didn't run into any bugs with this game and the progress is a good pace/straight forward. Hilarious, fast paced, and a good time.

I didn’t expect much when I started playing Fast Food Simulator, but it actually surprised me. The game is chaotic in the best way possible. Taking orders, cooking, cleaning, restocking, and trying not to completely lose control during rush hour somehow becomes really addictive.

The multiplayer aspect makes everything even funnier. Half of the fun is watching your friends panic while the kitchen turns into absolute disaster. It has that perfect balance between stressful and entertaining that keeps you saying “one more round.”

The graphics are simple but fit the vibe well, and the progression system gives you enough motivation to keep upgrading and improving your restaurant.

If you enjoy co-op games, management simulators, or just yelling at your friends over burnt burgers, this game is worth trying.

When people hear “fast food job,” their first thought is usually long hours, greasy kitchens, and endless customers. While those are elements of the job, there’s an often overlooked side that makes working in fast food surprisingly enjoyable and even rewarding i love my frickin chungus fast food sim
